LegalPrivacy.metaDescription: How Phoenix Global Holding processes personal data on its institutional website (Paraguay).
LegalPrivacy.title: Privacy policy
LegalPrivacy.intro: This policy explains what data we may collect, for what purposes, and what rights individuals have in connection with the Phoenix Global Holding institutional website. Processing complies with applicable Paraguayan law, in particular Law No. 1682/2001 on confidential information, and, where relevant, foreign rules depending on the territorial scope of each processing activity.

sections.heading: 1. Data controller and scope
paragraphs: The controller of personal data obtained through contact forms, institutional email or equivalent channels indicated on this site is Phoenix Global Holding, acting in coordination with its subsidiaries or affiliated companies when necessary to respond to your request or to comply with legal obligations.
This site is institutional and oriented to investors, partners and corporate clients; it is not a consumer retail store nor a services agreement until a specific written commercial contract exists.

sections.heading: 2. Categories of data
paragraphs: Professional identification and contact data: name, company, role, email, phone and country, when you voluntarily provide them.

Technical data generated while browsing: IP address, device type, browser, pages viewed, referral information and timestamps, collected through server logs or similar technologies required for security and site operation.

sections.heading: 3. Purposes and legal bases
paragraphs: To manage enquiries, requests for commercial or institutional information and pre-contractual relationships (basis: legitimate interest in responding to contact requests and, where applicable, pre-contractual measures).
To comply with legal obligations applicable in Paraguay or other jurisdictions when required (basis: legal obligation).
To maintain site security, prevent fraud and abuse, and improve usability through aggregated non-invasive analytics (basis: legitimate interests in security and service improvement).
Where a form includes an explicit acceptance box, certain communications may rely on consent, which may be withdrawn at any time without affecting prior lawful processing.

sections.heading: 4. Retention
paragraphs: Data are kept for as long as necessary to fulfil the purpose for which they were collected and thereafter for statutory limitation periods or regulatory requirements that apply.
Security logs may be retained for shorter or longer periods according to internal retention policies and cybersecurity requirements.

sections.heading: 5. Recipients and international transfers
paragraphs: We may share data with vendors providing hosting, email, analytics or cybersecurity services under agreements requiring confidentiality and processing consistent with this policy.
If any vendor processes data outside Paraguay, we will implement appropriate safeguards under applicable law (contractual clauses, binding corporate rules or other recognised mechanisms).

sections.heading: 6. Data subject rights
paragraphs: You may request access, rectification, updating or erasure of your personal data, and object to certain processing or request restriction, where applicable under Law No. 1682/2001 and related rules.

If you are in the European Economic Area or the United Kingdom and processing is subject to the GDPR or equivalent rules, you may exercise additional rights (portability, complaint to a supervisory authority) to the extent that regime applies.
To exercise your rights, use the institutional email shown in the contact section of this site and clearly describe your request. We may ask for reasonable information to verify your identity.

sections.heading: 7. Security
paragraphs: We apply technical and organisational measures proportionate to the risk: access controls, encryption where appropriate, internal confidentiality policies and periodic review of critical vendors.
No system is completely immune; if we detect an incident that seriously affects your data, we will act in accordance with applicable law, including mandatory notifications.

sections.heading: 8. Children
paragraphs: This site is not primarily directed at minors. If you believe we have collected a minor’s data without proper authorisation, contact us so we can delete it where the law allows.

sections.heading: 9. Cookies and similar technologies
paragraphs: The site may use strictly necessary technical cookies for operation and security. If non-essential analytics or third-party cookies are added, we will inform you and, where required, obtain prior consent under applicable law.
You may configure your browser to refuse cookies; some features may be limited.

sections.heading: 10. Changes and contact
paragraphs: We may update this policy to reflect legal or process changes. The current version will be published at this URL with an update date.
For privacy questions, use the email published in the institutional contact section of the website.

LegalTerms.metaTitle: Terms of use | Phoenix Global Holding
LegalTerms.metaDescription: Terms governing use of the Phoenix Global Holding institutional website.
LegalTerms.title: Website terms and conditions of use
LegalTerms.intro: Accessing and using this website means you accept these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the site. Phoenix Global Holding may modify these terms at any time.

sections.heading: 1. Site operator
paragraphs: The institutional website is operated by Phoenix Global Holding and its associated business divisions (including Phoenix Global Import and Export and Phoenix Global Developer), with significant presence and operations in the Republic of Paraguay and international reach in commercial and technology activities.

sections.heading: 2. Purpose and nature of information
paragraphs: Site content is for information and corporate presentation. It does not constitute legal, tax, financial or technical advice unless a specific written agreement is executed.
Descriptions of services, operations or capabilities are general and may not reflect particular agreements in force with third parties.

sections.heading: 3. Permitted use
paragraphs: You must not use the site in any way that violates the law, third-party rights, system security or the group’s reputation. Unauthorised large-scale scraping, malware introduction or unauthorised access attempts are prohibited.
You agree to provide accurate information in forms or contact channels and not to impersonate others.

sections.heading: 4. Intellectual property
paragraphs: Trademarks, logos, text, layouts, compilations and other site content are protected under applicable law. Reproduction, distribution or modification without prior written permission is prohibited, except private use or quotation for information as permitted by law.

sections.heading: 5. Disclaimer of warranties
paragraphs: The site is provided “as is” and “as available”. We do not warrant uninterrupted operation, absence of errors or vulnerabilities, or absolute accuracy of all content at all times.
Links to third-party sites are provided for convenience only; we do not control and are not responsible for their content or policies.

sections.heading: 6. Limitation of liability
paragraphs: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable Paraguayan law, Phoenix Global Holding and its affiliated companies shall not be liable for indirect damages, lost profits, data loss or harm arising from use or inability to use the site.
This limitation does not apply in cases of wilful misconduct, gross negligence or mandatory legal provisions that cannot be excluded.

sections.heading: 7. Governing law and jurisdiction
paragraphs: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Paraguay, without prejudice to mandatory rules of another state that may apply to consumers or specific situations.
Unless otherwise required by law, the parties submit to the competent courts of Asunción, Paraguay, for disputes arising from use of the site.

sections.heading: 8. Changes and contact
paragraphs: We may update these terms by publishing a new version on the site. Continued use after publication constitutes acceptance of material changes duly highlighted.
For questions about these terms, use the institutional email indicated in the contact section.
